title: PR to production: CI + CD
summary: Developer pushes a branch and opens a PR. GitHub Actions runs lint + build. Branch protection blocks merging main until the check is green. Once merged, Vercel auto-deploys main atomically. Clean split: GitHub Actions handles CI (gate before merge), Vercel handles CD (deploy after merge).

steps.label: git push -u origin feature
steps.note: Direct pushes to main are blocked by branch protection, so work always lands on a feature branch.

steps.label: open PR
steps.note: PR opened against main via the GitHub UI or gh CLI.

steps.label: trigger workflow
steps.note: Same workflow also runs on push to main as a safety net.

steps.label: npm ci
steps.note: Clean install from lockfile, cached by setup-node.

steps.label: npm run lint
steps.note: eslint via the next config — first thing that fails on bad code.

steps.label: npm run build
steps.note: Prisma generate + next build. A fake DATABASE_URL is enough because no queries run at build time — admin pages are force-dynamic and Home wraps DB calls in try/catch.

steps.label: report check status
steps.note: Green check unlocks the Merge button. Red check leaves the PR un-mergeable.

steps.label: click Merge
steps.note: Only available once branch protection's required checks pass.

steps.label: webhook: main updated
steps.note: Vercel's GitHub integration listens for pushes to main.

steps.label: prisma generate && next build
steps.note: Same build script as CI. Builds in Vercel's environment with the real DATABASE_URL + ADMIN_PASSWORD env vars.

steps.label: atomic deploy → live
steps.note: Zero-downtime swap. The previous deployment stays addressable as a preview URL for rollback.
